Does Clarinha speak English? Yes, she does.
Good evening, and welcome to Seoul. My name is James Lee. I am a professor at this university.
Good evening, Professor Lee. Nice to meet you. My name is Maria Clara. I work in education, in Brazil.
Wonderful. What do you do exactly, Maria Clara?
I am a professor, and I manage a team. I am responsible for international partnerships.
That is impressive. So you represent your institution here tonight?
Yes, I represent my university and a public council. I wear many hats.
I understand very well. Does your team travel with you?
No, my team stays in Brazil. This time, I travel alone, and I speak English on my own.
That is a big step. Your English is clear and calm. Do you have family in Brazil?
Yes. I have three children and one granddaughter. Her name is Clarinha, and she studies English too.
I am a professor, and I manage a team.
I work at a university and I represent my institution.
I wear many hats: professor, leader, and grandmother.
My granddaughter Clarinha studies English too.

Good evening, everyone, and welcome to our university in Seoul. My name is Grace, and I work in the international office. Tonight, we welcome guests from many countries. Our program is simple. First, we have a short welcome. Then, our rector says a few words. After that, we have dinner together in the main hall. Please feel at home. If you need help, our team is here for you. Thank you, and enjoy your evening.

Hello, Maria Clara. This is Daniel from the conference office. I have some information for you. The round table starts at ten o'clock, in Room Two. You speak after the professor from Japan. Each speaker has ten minutes. Please bring your name badge to the front desk before nine thirty. If you have a question, you can call me. Thank you, and have a good day.
Hello. My name is Maria Clara. I am a professor, and I manage a team. I am responsible for international partnerships.
